steps.title: Extract the vertex from vertex form
steps.content: The equation $y = -16(x - 6.8)^2 + 740$ is already written in vertex form. We can read the vertex $(6.8, 740)$ directly without expanding or solving.
steps.formula: $$y = a(x - h)^2 + k \text{ has vertex } (h, k)$$

steps.title: Interpret the vertex in context
steps.content: Here, $x$ is time in seconds after launch and $y$ is height in feet. Since $a = -16 < 0$, the parabola opens downward, making the vertex the maximum point. From the vertex $(6.8, 740)$: the maximum height is 740 feet, occurring at 6.8 seconds after launch. That matches choice B — B is correct.
